mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, persisted labor market slack reduces household income and consumption expectations, lowering corporate earnings forecasts and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day SPX return is positive in at least 60% of cases where UNRATE > 8.0%, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue growth and propels S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with month-over-month RSAFS changes (Spearman rank correlation < 0.1 over a 2-year rolling window), the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, aggressive monetary easing lowers discount rates and signals central bank support, triggering a short-term rally in equities over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of cases when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or if cumulative average return is not positive, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising industrial production and a very low federal funds rate signal a recovering supply side combined with accommodative monetary conditions, jointly boosting economic growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (INDPRO change × FEDFUNDS level) fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a significant positive coefficient in a multivariate regression (p>0.10), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-pandemic recovery phase, a rising nonfarm payroll count indicates improving labor market conditions, which supports consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If during the pandemic recovery period (2020M05-2021M12) the rank IC of PAYEMS change vs. 60-day forward returns is below 0.05, or if the signal does not outperform a random walk in directional accuracy (binomial test p>0.05), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which in turn signals broader economic strength and higher corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between INDPRO month-over-month growth and subsequent 20-day SPX return is not positive and statistically significant (Pearson r < 0.15, p>0.05 over 2-year rolling window), reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are low and the unemployment rate is elevated simultaneously, the combined effect of weak construction activity and high joblessness signals a depressed demand environment that depresses corporate profits, driving S&P 500 negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (low HOUST × high UNRATE) does not produce significantly negative 20-day returns (average return <-0.5% and one-sided t-test p<0.05), or if the signal is not monotonic across quartiles of the interaction, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment with rising inflation above the central bank's target, expectations of monetary tightening increase, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and erodes purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods where CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation exceeds 0.3% (annualized ~3.6%), the 5-day forward SPX return is not negative on average (one-sided t-test p>0.10), or if the negative signal does not outperform a random direction guess, reject hypothesis.
